segments.text: Am I not free? Am I not an apostle? Have I not seen Jesus our Lord? Are you not the result of my work in the Lord?
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Even though I may not be an apostle to others, surely I am to you! For you are the seal of my apostleship in the Lord.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: This is my defense to those who sit in judgment on me.
Don’t we have the right to food and drink?
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Don’t we have the right to take a believing wife along with us, as do the other apostles and the Lord’s brothers and Cephas?
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Or is it only I and Barnabas who lack the right to not work for a living?
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Who serves as a soldier at his own expense? Who plants a vineyard and does not eat its grapes? Who tends a flock and does not drink the milk?
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Do I say this merely on human authority? Doesn’t the Law say the same thing?
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: For it is written in the Law of Moses:
Do not muzzle an ox while it is treading out the grain.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Is it about oxen that God is concerned?
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Surely he says this for us, doesn’t he? Yes, this was written for us, because whoever plows and threshes should be able to do so in the hope of sharing in the harvest.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: If we have sown spiritual seed among you, is it too much if we reap a material harvest from you?
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: If others have this right of support from you, shouldn’t we have it all the more? But we did not use this right. On the contrary, we put up with anything rather than hinder the gospel of Christ.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Don’t you know that those who serve in the temple get their food from the temple, and that those who serve at the altar share in what is offered on the altar?
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: In the same way, the Lord has commanded that those who preach the gospel should receive their living from the gospel.
But I have not used any of these rights. And I am not writing this in the hope that you will do such things for me, for I would rather die than allow anyone to deprive me of this boast.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: For when I preach the gospel, I cannot boast, since I am compelled to preach. Woe to me if I do not preach the gospel!
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: If I preach voluntarily, I have a reward; if not voluntarily, I am simply discharging the trust committed to me.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: What then is my reward? Just this: that in preaching the gospel I may offer it free of charge, and so not make full use of my rights as a preacher of the gospel.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Though I am free and belong to no one, I have made myself a slave to everyone, to win as many as possible.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: To the Jews I became like a Jew, to win the Jews. To those under the law I became like one under the law (though I myself am not under the law), so as to win those under the law.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: To those not having the law I became like one not having the law (though I am not free from God’s law but am under Christ’s law), so as to win those not having the law.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: To the weak I became weak, to win the weak. I have become all things to all people so that by all possible means I might save some.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: I do all this for the sake of the gospel, that I may share in its blessings.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Do you not know that in a race all the runners run, but only one gets the prize? Run in such a way as to get the prize.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Everyone who competes in the games goes into strict training. They do it to get a crown that will not last, but we do it to get a crown that will last forever.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Therefore I do not run like someone running aimlessly; I do not fight like a boxer beating the air.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: No, I strike a blow to my body and make it my slave so that after I have preached to others, I myself will not be disqualified for the prize.
segments.speaker: Paul
